channel.title: Posts on Gertm's blog
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Gertm's blog

item.title: Interests or career building?
item.description: I’ve been playing with Prolog in my spare time for a while now. It’s good fun, makes me think differently about programming and sometimes hurts my brain thinking about the way it works. But does this actually help me for my career? Should it matter that it helps me for my career? Talking to colleagues and friends, I hear a lot of different opinions. "Free time is for learning new frameworks and libraries so you can do better in your next job.

item.title: Running Swi Prolog Http Server Behind Nginx
item.description: This post will remind me how to run the SWI-Prolog http server behind an nginx reverse proxy. This assumes experience with nginx and already have a server up and running. SWI-Prolog has a built-in webserver in library(http/http_server). Of course exposing this kind of thing directly to the internet is never a good idea and most people put nginx in front of it.

item.title: Another blog reboot
item.description: Trying this again. Now it’ll be hosted on Github pages, which means it’ll survive my umpteenth VPS reinstall. I could also host this myself, but the VPS doesn’t allow too much traffic and doing it on Github pages is actually really convenient.

item.title: Why I stopped using Linux
item.description: Update 2019-02-10: This is no longer true since I've recently installed Linux on my laptop again and I'm quite happy with it. We'll see how long it lasts. TL;DR: Updating video drivers screwed my system once too many. Reinstalled to Windows and never looked back. Now the PC works for me, instead of me working to keep it running. As most people of my age, I started out on my parent’s computer.
